commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: sword: So also Isaiah says, "And he hath set my mouth as a sharp sword;" and the Apostle, "And the sword of the Spirit, which is the Word of God."

commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: winepress: It is even so. For now also He treads until He tread without the city. <a

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Sharp two-edged sword. The power which Christ exercises over the impious. (Menochius)
Wine press This painting corresponds to the triumph of Jesus Christ. (Calmet)
Some of the attributes here mentioned are also ascribed to Christ, by the prophet Isaiah, (Isaias lxiii. 2, 3.) "Why then is thy apparel red, and thy garments like theirs that tread in the wine press? I have trodden the wine-press alone, and of the Gentiles there is not a man with me: I have trampled on them in my indignation, and have trodden them down in my wrath, and their blood is sprinkled upon my garments, and I have stained all my apparel. "(Pastorini)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
